entries.note: Adani Enterprises executed a QIP at a floor price of ₹3,034.68 per share in July 2026, serving as a large-cap conglomerate benchmark for institutional ECM appetite during a broader market recovery phase.

Prudential received IRDAI approval in July 2026 to commence a joint venture health insurance operation with HCL, establishing it as a new institutionally-backed private health insurer in India with medium-term growth capital and potential IPO requirements.

HCL Health Insurance is a greenfield private health insurer established via a joint venture with Prudential, receiving IRDAI regulatory approval in July 2026; the entity is expected to require growth equity rounds and is a prospective IPO candidate over the medium term.

Social Worth Technologies, the parent entity of the Fibe (formerly EarlySalary) lending-tech platform, filed a DRHP with SEBI in July 2026 for a ₹750 crore fresh-issue IPO, with an OFS component indicating PE and early-stage investor exit intent in the NBFC-fintech segment.

Social Worth Technologies, the parent entity of consumer-credit fintech brands Fibe and EarlySalary, filed a DRHP in mid-2026 for an IPO comprising a ₹750 crore fresh issue plus an OFS of up to 4 crore equity shares, marking one of the first digital-lending NBFC listings under the tightened NBFC regulatory regime.

Social Worth Technologies, the parent company of consumer-lending fintech brand Fibe, filed a DRHP with SEBI in mid-2026 for a ₹750 cr fresh-issue IPO (plus an OFS component), with fresh proceeds earmarked primarily for capital infusion into its lending subsidiary ESPL; the filing marked one of the first major digital consumer-lending NBFC IPO filings of the FY27 season.

PB Fintech, the listed insurance-tech and lending marketplace platform, saw a 2.6% block sale in July 2026, providing secondary price-discovery data for institutional demand depth in the BFSI fintech segment at prevailing valuations.

Prism, the holding entity of Oyo, filed a UDRHP-I with SEBI in July 2026 for an all-fresh-issue mainboard IPO of ₹6,650 crore aimed primarily at debt reduction; as one of the largest near-term primary market filings it is a significant QIB bandwidth consumer with implications for concurrent BFSI deal timing.

Prism, the holding entity of hospitality-tech platform Oyo, re-filed an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-I) in mid-2026 for an all-fresh-issue IPO of ₹6,650 crore, its second SEBI filing after an earlier DRHP approval had lapsed; the entirely fresh-issue structure indicates capital will be deployed into operations rather than promoter liquidity.

Prism, the renamed parent entity of Oyo, filed an Updated Draft Red Herring Prospectus (UDRHP-I) with SEBI in July 2026 for a ₹6,650 cr entirely fresh-issue IPO, with proceeds designated for debt repayment; the filing followed an earlier DRHP approval and represents a large-cap consumer-tech primary market test.

SEBI proposed regulations in mid-2026 restricting celebrity endorsements for investment schemes, drawing industry pushback; the outcome of this consultation is expected to reshape retail IPO marketing strategies and affect intermediary distribution models for primary issuances.

SEBI granted approval for the SBI Funds Management IPO in 2026 and introduced the SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations 2026, effective April 1, 2026, which introduced fee-compression measures flagged as a material profitability risk factor in the SBI MF prospectus and applicable across the Indian AMC industry.

SEBI issued a circular in July 2026 tightening the obligations of trading members with respect to clients' unpaid securities, reinforcing settlement discipline and carrying potential implications for margin financing, short-selling, and deferred-payment mechanics in IPO anchor and QIB allocation processes.

RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra affirmed in July 2026 that India will not raise its 4% inflation target, reinforcing a credible long-term monetary framework that supports stable long-end bond yields and provides a predictable pricing environment for AT1, Tier-2, and long-dated sub-debt issuances by BFSI entities.

The RBI announced in 2026 that banks will be permitted to lend directly to SEBI-registered REITs and InvITs effective April 2027, expanding the bank lending universe into alternative asset vehicles and creating new refinancing pathways that sponsors can pre-position for through capital structure planning and follow-on ECM activity.

SBI Funds Management completed an IPO in July 2026 (launching week of July 13, listing July 22) in which State Bank of India divested a 6.3% stake and Amundi divested 3.7%, targeting a valuation of approximately ₹1.17 trillion; the deal was structured as a pure OFS with no fresh-issue component and ranked as the largest AMC IPO in Indian primary-market history at the time.

SBI Funds Management, India's largest AMC by AUM (₹16.32 tn as of early 2026), completed a ₹11,416 cr IPO in July 2026 via a pure OFS of ~20.37 cr shares, with SBI divesting 6.3% and Amundi India Holding divesting 3.7%, targeting a valuation of approximately $12.3 bn; the IPO received SEBI approval and listed on July 22, 2026.

State Bank of India is the majority promoter of SBI Funds Management and divested a 6.3% stake via the SBI Funds Management IPO in July 2026, reducing its holding while retaining majority ownership of one of India's largest asset managers.

State Bank of India, as the majority promoter of SBI Funds Management, divested a 6.3% stake via the SBI Funds Management IPO in July 2026, representing one of the largest promoter OFS transactions in the Indian AMC sector.

Amundi, the French asset-management group, holds a strategic minority stake in SBI Funds Management and divested 3.7% via the July 2026 IPO, participating in the largest AMC listing in Indian primary-market history.
Amundi India Holding, the Indian holding vehicle of French asset manager Amundi, divested a 3.7% stake in SBI Funds Management via the July 2026 IPO OFS, reducing its exposure in India's largest AMC.

Laser Power & Infra, a Kolkata-based infrastructure issuer, downsized its IPO to ₹742 crore (₹542 crore fresh issue plus ₹200 crore OFS) and opened for subscription July 9–13, 2026; the size reduction from an earlier larger filing and explicit promoter objective to retire ₹490 crore of debt signal deleveraging as the primary use of proceeds.

Aerion Energy Solutions received board approval in July 2026 to raise up to ₹10,000 crore via a Qualified Institutional Placement, one of the larger QIP board mandates seen in the Indian market that year, with proceeds intended for energy-transition capital deployment.

HDFC Bank reported gross advances of ₹30.61 lakh crore (+15.4% YoY) and deposits of +14.7% YoY in its Q1 FY27 business update, demonstrating sustained post-merger balance sheet momentum and serving as a key sentiment indicator for BFSI sector primary issuance windows.

The Government of India holds a majority stake in Cochin Shipyard and conducted a 5.04% OFS in July 2026 over two days, making it a benchmark PSU divestment transaction for gauging institutional appetite for government paper in the secondary market.

national-stock-exchange-of-india.name: National Stock Exchange of India Limited

entries.note: NSE filed its DRHP for an IPO in 2026 following SEBI's no-objection certificate and board approval in February 2026, positioning the listing as potentially one of the largest IPOs in Indian capital markets history and the most significant market-infrastructure ECM transaction to date.
